chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ الْمُضَارَبَةِ وَ مَا لَهُ مِنَ الرِّبْحِ وَ مَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْوَضِيعَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Guarantor of the speculation and what is for him from the profit and what is against him from the loss

text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) فِي الْمُضَارِبِ مَا أَنْفَقَ فِي سَفَرِهِ فَهُوَ مِنْ جَمِيعِ الْمَالِ وَ إِذَا قَدِمَ بَلْدَتَهُ فَمَا أَنْفَقَ فَهُوَ مِنْ نَصِيبِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said regarding the speculative profit-sharing: ‘Whatever he spends in his journey so it would be from the entirety of the wealth (as valid deductible expenditure), but when he proceeds back to his city, so whatever he spends, so it would be from his own share’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, about Mudarabah (profit sharing), has said, ‘Whatever he spends during his journey is from the whole of the capital, but when he is back home then whatever he spends is from his own share.’”
